29.       - Identified property function performing file I/O during evaluation phase
30.       - Explained difference between MSBuild evaluation phase and execution phase

data.content:
Here are the MSBuild evaluation performance issues in this project:

---

### 1. Deep Import Chain (`level1 → level2 → level3`)

The `.csproj` imports `level1.props`, which imports `level2.props`, which imports `level3.props`. Each import must be resolved, parsed, and evaluated during the **evaluation phase** — the phase where MSBuild reads all project files, resolves properties, and expands items *before* any targets run. Deeply nested imports increase evaluation time linearly and make the dependency graph harder to cache. Flatten these into a single `.props` file or reduce nesting where possible.

### 2. Overly Broad Glob Pattern (`**\*.*`)

```xml
<AdditionalFiles Include="**\*.*" Exclude="**\bin\**;**\obj\**" />
```

This glob scans **every file in every subdirectory** of the project root during evaluation. It will traverse into `.git/`, `node_modules/`, `packages/`, and any other large directory trees — potentially enumerating thousands of files. Only `bin` and `obj` are excluded.

**Fix:** Restrict the glob to specific extensions or directories, and leverage `DefaultItemExcludes` (which the SDK already sets to exclude `.git`, etc.):

```xml
<AdditionalFiles Include="**\*.cs;**\*.json" />
```

Or add broader exclusions explicitly.

### 3. File I/O via Property Function During Evaluation

```xml
<BuildNotes Condition="Exists('build-notes.txt')">$([System.IO.File]::ReadAllText('build-notes.txt'))</BuildNotes>
```

This calls `System.IO.File.ReadAllText()` **during evaluation**, not during target execution. Property functions are evaluated eagerly, meaning this performs disk I/O every time the project is evaluated (design-time builds, IDE loads, restores, etc.). This is expensive and blocks the evaluation pipeline.

**Fix:** Move file reading into a target that runs at build time:

```xml
<Target Name="ReadBuildNotes" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <ReadLinesFromFile File="build-notes.txt" Condition="Exists('build-notes.txt')">
    <Output TaskParameter="Lines" PropertyName="BuildNotes" />
  </ReadLinesFromFile>
</Target>
```

---

### Summary

| Issue | Phase | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| 3-level deep import chain | Evaluation | Extra file parsing per evaluation pass |
| `**\*.*` glob | Evaluation | Full recursive directory scan |
| `File::ReadAllText` property function | Evaluation | Synchronous disk I/O on every evaluation |

The **evaluation phase** runs far more often than builds (IDEs re-evaluate on every file change), so all three issues have an outsized impact on responsiveness.
